title: PowerShell Microsoft.Graph SDK - Authentication And SharePoint Operations
applies_to: Unattended PowerShell scripts calling Microsoft Graph
Writing data to SharePoint lists from PowerShell
Uploading files to SharePoint document libraries from PowerShell
Azure Automation runbooks using Graph API

confirmed_facts.fact: The Microsoft.Graph PowerShell SDK v2 is the current recommended module. Install the full umbrella module with `Install-Module Microsoft.Graph -Scope CurrentUser` or individual sub-modules such as `Microsoft.Graph.Sites`, `Microsoft.Graph.Files`, and `Microsoft.Graph.Authentication` to reduce footprint.

For app-only (unattended) authentication, Connect-MgGraph supports three methods: client secret via ClientSecretCredential, X.509 certificate via -CertificateThumbprint or -CertificateName, and Managed Identity via -Identity when running on Azure-hosted compute. Certificate-based is preferred for production; Managed Identity is preferred when running in Azure Automation or Azure Functions.

Client secret authentication pattern: `$credential = New-Object System.Net.NetworkCredential($clientId, (ConvertTo-SecureString $clientSecret -AsPlainText -Force)); Connect-MgGraph -TenantId $tenantId -ClientSecretCredential $credential`. Requires Microsoft.Graph.Authentication module.

Certificate-based app-only auth: `Connect-MgGraph -TenantId $tenantId -ClientId $appId -CertificateThumbprint $thumbprint`. The certificate must be installed in the local machine or current user certificate store, and the public key must be uploaded to the app registration in Entra ID.

Managed Identity auth (Azure Automation, Azure Functions): `Connect-MgGraph -Identity`. No credential management needed. The Managed Identity must be granted the required Microsoft Graph application permissions via `New-MgServicePrincipalAppRoleAssignment`.

SharePoint site ID resolution: `$site = Get-MgSite -SiteId "$tenantName.sharepoint.com:/sites/$siteName:"`. The SiteId format uses the host:path URL encoding with a trailing colon. Returns the site object with the canonical `id` property needed for subsequent calls.

List discovery: `Get-MgSiteList -SiteId $siteId` returns all lists on a site. Filter by display name: `Get-MgSiteList -SiteId $siteId -Filter "displayName eq 'MyList'"`. Returns the list `id` required for item operations.

Retrieve all list items (with paging handled automatically): `Get-MgSiteListItem -SiteId $siteId -ListId $listId -All -ExpandProperty fields`. Without `-All`, only the first page (up to 200 items by default) is returned. Without `-ExpandProperty fields`, field values are not included.

Create a list item: `New-MgSiteListItem -SiteId $siteId -ListId $listId -Fields @{ Title = 'value'; CustomColumn = 'value' }`. The Fields hashtable must use internal (programmatic) column names, not display names.

Update a list item: `Update-MgSiteListItem -SiteId $siteId -ListId $listId -ListItemId $itemId -Fields @{ CustomColumn = 'new value' }`. Only include the fields being changed; omitted fields are left unchanged.

Small file upload to SharePoint document library (< 4MB): get the drive ID with `Get-MgSiteDrive -SiteId $siteId`, then upload with `Set-MgDriveItemContent -DriveId $driveId -DriveItemId "root:/$fileName:" -InFile $localFilePath`. For files > 4MB, create an upload session with `New-MgDriveItemUploadSession`.

Use `-NoWelcome` with Connect-MgGraph in automated scripts to suppress the interactive consent prompt and welcome banner: `Connect-MgGraph -TenantId $tenantId -ClientId $appId -CertificateThumbprint $thumbprint -NoWelcome`.

implementation_notes: Resolve $siteId and $listId once in a discovery run and cache them as constants in the script rather than resolving on every execution. Site and list IDs are stable and do not change.

Prefer individual sub-modules over the umbrella Microsoft.Graph module to reduce installation size and import time. For SharePoint work, `Microsoft.Graph.Sites` and `Microsoft.Graph.Files` are sufficient.

Discover internal column names before writing: `Get-MgSiteListColumn -SiteId $siteId -ListId $listId | Select-Object DisplayName, Name` — the `Name` property is the internal name to use in Fields hashtables.

For Azure Automation, use `-Identity` with Managed Identity and assign Graph application permissions using `New-MgServicePrincipalAppRoleAssignment` targeting the Automation Account's service principal.
After Connect-MgGraph, always call `Get-MgContext` to verify the connected tenant and scopes before executing sensitive operations.
caveats: Internal column names differ from display names and may contain underscores or encoded characters. Always verify with Get-MgSiteListColumn before first write.
Microsoft.Graph SDK v2 requires PowerShell 5.1+ on Windows or PowerShell 7.2+ cross-platform. PowerShell 7.2 LTS is recommended.
The `-All` switch retrieves all pages but can be slow for large lists. For lists with thousands of items, use `-Filter` to narrow the query before paginating.
Application permissions (Sites.ReadWrite.All) grant access to all sites in the tenant. Use delegated permissions or site-scoped app registration for tighter scope in production.
ClientSecretCredential constructor syntax varies between SDK versions. Prefer certificate-based or Managed Identity auth to avoid version compatibility issues.
